meta.title: Unit 3 — Pronouns & I/me (Test 2)
meta.subject: English Grammar
meta.description: Practice choosing the right pronoun to replace nouns in sentences.

questions.question: Which pronoun replaces a thing or animal?
questions.explanation: "it" is used to replace a thing or animal.

questions.question: "They" is a plural pronoun.
questions.explanation: Yes! "they" replaces a group of people or things — it's a plural pronoun.

questions.question: Which sentence is correct?
questions.explanation: "I go to school." — "I" is the subject and comes before the verb.

questions.question: "Me" is used after verbs (as the object).
questions.explanation: Yes! "me" is the object pronoun — it comes after verbs. e.g., "Rosa calls me."

questions.question: Do you hear the wolves? ___ are howling.
questions.explanation: "wolves" is plural — we use "They" to replace multiple animals.

questions.question: Fill in: ___ live in the ranch. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it comes before the verb. "I live in the ranch."

questions.question: Amy goes to the park. → ___ touches the grass.
questions.explanation: Amy is a female — we use "She" to replace Amy's name.

questions.question: Sort these pronouns: Singular or Plural?
questions.explanation: Note: 'you' can be both! he/it = singular. we/they = plural.

questions.question: In the sentence "My mom calls me every day," the pronoun "me" is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "me" comes after the verb (object). My mom calls me — "me" is the receiver.

questions.question: Fill in: It gives ___ an idea. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "me" is the object — it comes after the verb. "It gives me an idea."

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "She sees a cute camel." 'She' is the subject pronoun.

questions.question: This is Tanya. ___ has purple hair.
questions.explanation: Tanya is female — we use "She" to replace Tanya's name.

questions.question: In the sentence "Can you give I the pencil?," the pronoun is correct.
questions.explanation: After a verb, we use "me" (the object): "Can you give me the pencil?"

questions.question: Mia and I work together. → ___ do an art project.
questions.explanation: "Mia and I" = two people (including the speaker) → use "We".

questions.question: Fill in: ___ am not scared of the lion. (I or me)
questions.explanation: "I" is the subject — it goes before the verb. "I am not scared of the lion."

questions.question: Sort these pronouns by use: Subject (before verb) or Object (after verb)?
questions.explanation: I/he/she are subject pronouns. me is an object pronoun (comes after the verb).

questions.question: Which sentence uses 'I' and 'me' correctly?
questions.explanation: "I love dogs and cats." — "I" is the subject before the verb.

questions.question: In the sentence "Spotty is my dog. It likes to play fetch," the pronoun "It" is correct.
questions.explanation: Yes! "It" replaces "Spotty" (a dog = animal). We use "it" for animals when gender is not specified.

questions.question: Arrange the words to form a correct sentence:
questions.explanation: Correct: "Mommy and I put our clothes in it." 'I' = subject, 'it' = object.

questions.question: Justin looks for his pencil. ___ finds the pencil.
questions.explanation: Justin is male — we use "He" to replace Justin's name.
